etymology.description: 聿(聿) (yù, "writing brush") + 月(月) (yuè, "flesh; meat") = phonetic element suggesting sound "ruǎn" -> final form 𦘒
etymology.interpretation: The character 𦘒 combines 聿(聿) (yù, "writing brush") for its phonetic component, suggesting the sound "ruǎn," with 月(月) (yuè, "flesh; meat") to possibly represent the notion of softness associated with flesh, aligning with the meaning of "soft."
